---
title: "Minku Kang"
pagetitle: "Minku Kang — Preventive medicine physician & health-policy researcher"
description: "Minku Kang (강민구) — preventive medicine physician, social epidemiologist, and health-policy researcher in Seoul."
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

<style>#title-block-header{display:none}</style>

<div class="mk-wrap">

<section class="mk-hero">
<div class="mk-hgrid">
<div>
<h1 class="mk-name">Minku Kang<span class="mk-kr">강민구</span></h1>
<p class="mk-lead">I study how work, inequality, and social policy shape mental health — and turn the evidence into reform.</p>
</div>
<figure class="mk-portrait">
<img src="images/profile.jpg" alt="Minku Kang">
<figcaption>Preventive medicine physician<br>Armed Forces Medical Command, Seoul</figcaption>
</figure>
</div>
</section>

<section class="mk-about">
<p>Minku Kang is a preventive medicine physician and social epidemiologist. His research asks how precarious work, long working hours, and inequality translate into poor mental health — and how that evidence can drive institutional reform.</p>
<p>As president of the Korean Intern Resident Association, he led the national effort to reform resident duty-hour regulations, for which he received a commendation from the Minister of Health and Welfare. He is completing a PhD in Public Health at Korea University and a Master of Public Policy at the KDI School, and serves as an epidemiologist and health-policy officer at the Armed Forces Medical Command.</p>
<p>He also works to turn preventive medicine into care that people can actually receive — as vice president and a founding member of the Korean Association of Preventive Medicine Physicians, and as a board member of the Korean Society for Preventive Medicine, where he chairs the Future Generations Committee and serves on the Clinical Preventive Medicine Committee.</p>
<details class="mk-bio">
<summary>Full biography</summary>
<p>I graduated from Korea University with an M.D. and a B.A. in Sociology with a minor in Economics, and received an M.P.P. from the KDI School. During my undergraduate years, I served as the Vice President of the Korea University Student Union, where I actively engaged in university and community governance, focusing on youth housing and education policy issues. Following my graduation, I completed my medical internship and residency in Preventive Medicine at the Korea University Medical Center. Concurrently, I embarked on a Ph.D. program in Public Health at Korea University to build a strong analytical foundation for public health research.</p>
<p>During my residency, I became deeply involved in health policy and healthcare workforce advocacy. I served as the President of the Korean Intern Resident Association (KIRA), where I led efforts to improve working conditions and mental health for young doctors. I acted as the Principal Investigator for the national study on reforming the 36-hour consecutive duty system, and my efforts contributed to crucial reforms in resident working-hour regulations. For these contributions, I received a commendation from the Minister of Health and Welfare. I also represented Korean junior doctors internationally as a delegate for the World Medical Association – Junior Doctors Network (WMA-JDN).</p>
<p>After obtaining my board certification in Preventive Medicine, I began my military service. I am currently serving as an Epidemiologist and Healthcare Management Officer at the Armed Forces Medical Command (AFMC). In this capacity, I manage public mental health and infectious disease prevention initiatives and lead research projects assessing the mental health and healthcare utilization of military personnel. Previously, I contributed to public health crisis management at the COVID-19 Community Treatment Center in Seoul and served as a visiting researcher at the National Center for Mental Health.</p>
<p>My primary research lies at the intersection of social epidemiology and social policy. I develop methodological and applied research to understand how social inequalities — such as precarious employment, long working hours, and area deprivation — translate into adverse health and mental health outcomes. My recent publications have addressed gender inequalities in mental health during the COVID-19 pandemic and the impact of national health screening programs. I am currently completing my doctoral dissertation, which extensively analyzes working conditions, sleep deprivation, and depressive symptoms among Korean medical residents.</p>
<p>Beyond research, I am deeply committed to community medicine and institutional reform. I currently serve as a Board Member and the Chairperson of the Future Generations Committee for the Korean Society for Preventive Medicine (KSPM), as well as the Vice President and Founding Member of the Korean Association of Preventive Medicine Physicians (KAPMP). Through these roles, and my participation in multiple Ministry of Health and Welfare advisory committees, I continue to strive for practical, preventive approaches to resolve structural issues and build a cohesive society where diverse individuals can thrive together.</p>
</details>
</section>

<section class="mk-sec">
<div class="mk-lbl">Selected work</div>
<div class="mk-list">
<a href="research.qmd"><span class="y">2022–25</span><span class="t">Reform of the resident 36-hour consecutive-duty system</span><span class="m">Principal investigator</span></a>
<a href="research.qmd"><span class="y">2026–</span><span class="t">Determinants of mental health among military personnel</span><span class="m">Principal investigator</span></a>
<a href="publications.qmd"><span class="y">2023</span><span class="t">Gender inequalities in pandemic-era mental health</span><span class="m">J. Prev. Med. Public Health</span></a>
</div>
</section>

<section class="mk-sec">
<div class="mk-lbl">Focus</div>
<div class="mk-themes">
<div class="mk-theme"><h3>Preventive medicine</h3><p>Clinical preventive medicine, health-risk assessment, screening and management, population and occupational medicine, lifestyle medicine.</p></div>
<div class="mk-theme"><h3>Social determinants of health</h3><p>Workplace mental health, suicide prevention, social epidemiology, labour markets, health inequalities, and the neighbourhood environment.</p></div>
<div class="mk-theme"><h3>Health policy &amp; management</h3><p>Healthcare workers, health systems, and healthcare reform.</p></div>
</div>
</section>

<section class="mk-contact">
<div class="mk-lbl">Contact</div>
<a class="mk-mail" href="mailto:kangminku.kr@gmail.com">kangminku.kr@gmail.com</a>
<div class="mk-links">
<a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=BUn--4YAAAAJ" target="_blank">Google Scholar</a>
<a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-7226-8159" target="_blank">ORCID</a>
<a href="https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Minku-Kang-2" target="_blank">ResearchGate</a>
<a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/kangminku/" target="_blank">LinkedIn</a>
<a href="assets/CV_Minku_Kang_2026.pdf" target="_blank">Curriculum Vitae (PDF)</a>
</div>
</section>

</div>

<script>document.body.classList.add('mk-home');</script>
